heading: Quality improvement program for hospitals with a high severity adjusted readmission rate
label: 280j–3 Quality improvement program for hospitals with a high severity adjusted readmission rate

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Secretary shall make available a program for eligible hospitals to improve their readmission rates through the use of patient safety organizations (as defined in ).March 23, 2010section 299b–21(4) of this title

children.heading: Eligible hospital defined
children.label: 2 Eligible hospital defined
children.text: In this subsection, the term “eligible hospital” means a hospital that the Secretary determines has a high rate of risk adjusted readmissions for the conditions described in and has not taken appropriate steps to reduce such readmissions and improve patient safety as evidenced through historically high rates of readmissions, as determined by the Secretary.section 1395ww(q)(8)(A) of this title

children.heading: Risk adjustment
children.label: 3 Risk adjustment
children.text: The Secretary shall utilize appropriate risk adjustment measures to determine eligible hospitals.

children.heading: Report to the Secretary
children.label: b Report to the Secretary
children.text: As determined appropriate by the Secretary, eligible hospitals and patient safety organizations working with those hospitals shall report to the Secretary on the processes employed by the hospital to improve readmission rates and the impact of such processes on readmission rates.
